title: hieracium murorum subsp. eurygonium
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. eurygonium, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. eurygonium include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting healthy digestion, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Hieracium murorum subsp. eurygonium can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for oral consumption, and it can also be applied topically as a salve or poultice for skin issues.
